comment:
Hardware probe: bypass state[7]. The v1 / v1.5 SyncProbe family invoked state[24] successfully but the SHL-22 plus state[7] normalize-and-postbox chain dropped every return to a constant near zero. This v2 patches the same LineSel-cloned knob1 blob TWICE: state[31] -> state[24] at +0x65 (same as v1), and at +0x80..+0xab the SHL/state[7] tail-call is REPLACED with a direct STW.D1T1 A4,*+A0[5] that writes state[24]'s raw uint32 return into params[5], then pops B3 and returns. No UI postbox sync, no float normalization, no SHL. params[5] is whatever state[24] hands back.

What to watch on hardware:
  1. Load: does the unbypassed audio pass through?
  2. Edit-mode open: does the knob view stay alive?
  3. Turn the Sync knob: does the audio gain change at all?
  4. Press the left knob (tap tempo) at a steady ~120 BPM with Sync at any non-OFF value:
     does the gain pulse / change as the BPM changes?

The audio body maps the bottom 8 bits of params[5] (interpreted as a uint32 bit
pattern, NOT a float) to a 0..2 linear gain. So:
  state[24] return = 0       -> silent
  state[24] return small     -> quiet
  state[24] return large     -> loud, plus 256-step periodic wrap-around
A value that tracks BPM should be audible as a continuously varying gain.
gid_comment: 0x02 = Filter category. Same as the v1 SyncProbe family.
fxid_comment: 0x019E. New FXID so it sits next to SyncPrB4 / SyncPrB6 / SyncPrBA in the browser.

params.name: Sync
params.flags_comment: 0x28 — same Pattern B tempo-sync bit as the v1 SyncProbes.
params.comment: Single Sync slot. The bypass-state[7] handler writes state[24]'s raw uint32 return directly here.
